# 🎤 Voice-Based Concept Understanding Analyser (VBCUA)

Evaluate how effectively a speaker understands and explains a conceptual topic — from a single spoken recording.

VBCUA combines **Whisper transcription**, **Sentence-BERT semantic similarity**, and **Librosa audio analysis** to score spoken explanations for conceptual accuracy and delivery quality, then packages everything into a downloadable PDF report.

---

## ✨ Features

- **Concept-based evaluation** — pick a concept (e.g. *Cloud Computing*) and compare a spoken explanation against a built-in reference answer.
- **Speech-to-text transcription** using OpenAI's Whisper.
- **Semantic similarity scoring** via Sentence-BERT, measuring how closely the transcript matches the reference explanation.
- **Fluency & delivery analysis** with Librosa — filler-word ratio, pause ratio, RMS energy, and audio duration.
- **Waveform visualization** of the uploaded recording.
- **Automatic classification** (e.g. *Strong Understanding*) based on the combined score.
- **Qualitative feedback** — plain-language bullet points on clarity, pacing, and concept alignment.
- **Downloadable PDF report** summarizing the full evaluation.

---

---

## 🧰 Tech Stack

| Component | Technology |
|---|---|
| Frontend / App framework | [Streamlit](https://streamlit.io) |
| Speech-to-text | [OpenAI Whisper](https://github.com/openai/whisper) |
| Semantic similarity | [Sentence-Transformers](https://www.sbert.net) (Sentence-BERT) |
| Audio analysis | [Librosa](https://librosa.org) |
| PDF report generation | ReportLab |
| Language | Python 3.9+ |

---

## 🚀 Getting Started

### 1. Install FFmpeg (required by Whisper)

```bash
# Windows
choco install ffmpeg

# macOS
brew install ffmpeg

# Linux
sudo apt-get install ffmpeg
```

### 2. Clone / open the project folder

```bash
cd VBCUA
```

### 3. Create and activate a virtual environment

```bash
python3 -m venv vbcu_env

# macOS / Linux
source vbcu_env/bin/activate

# Windows
vbcu_env\Scripts\activate
```

### 4. Install dependencies

```bash
pip install --upgrade pip
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

> This pulls in Torch, Whisper, and Sentence-Transformers, so it can take a few minutes and needs a few GB of disk space.

### 5. (Optional) Run the tests

```bash
pytest test_vbcua.py -v
```

### 6. Launch the app

```bash
streamlit run app.py
```

The app opens in your browser at **http://localhost:8501**.

---

## 🖱️ Usage

1. **Select a Concept** — choose from the dropdown (e.g. *Cloud Computing*) and optionally review the reference explanation.
2. **Upload Your Spoken Explanation** — upload a `.wav` recording and click **Analyze Explanation**.
3. **View Evaluation Results** — see your Final Score, Semantic Similarity, Fluency Score, Classification, transcript, and waveform.
4. **Review Detailed Metrics & Feedback** — check filler words, pause ratio, RMS energy, and audio duration, alongside qualitative feedback.
5. **Download Report** — export everything as a PDF.

### Notes

- Only `.wav` files work out of the box. Convert other formats with:
  ```bash
  ffmpeg -i input.mp3 -ar 16000 -ac 1 output.wav
  ```
- The first run downloads the Whisper and Sentence-BERT models, so an internet connection is required then (subsequent runs use the cached models).
- On CPU-only hardware, use Whisper's `tiny` or `base` model size (set in the sidebar) for faster processing.

---

## 📁 Project Structure

```
VBCUA/
├── app.py                # Main Streamlit application
├── requirements.txt      # Python dependencies
├── test_vbcua.py         # Test suite
├── README.md             # This file
└── ...                   # Supporting modules (transcription, scoring, PDF export, etc.)
```

---

## 🗺️ Roadmap

- Support for additional languages beyond English
- Speaker diarization for group discussions
- User authentication and multi-user analysis history
- Expanded concept library
